title: Insertion and deletion together in a Heap

content.problemStatement:
Write a program to perform the following operations:


Initialize an empty array
Given an integer N, perform N queries over this array
Given a query "+ x", insert x in your integer array at the end.
Given a query "-", remove the (Single occurrence of)maximum element of the array.
After each query print the maximum element left in the array.
Note:
For the given test cases the array will never be empty.

Input Format

The first line of each test case contains one integer N, the number of queries.
There will be N more lines, each line giving one query.


Output Format

Print the maximum element left in the array, after each query.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N, the number of queries. The next N lines contain a query either of the form "+ x" (insert integer x) or "-" (delete one occurrence of the current maximum element). It is guaranteed that after every query the array is non‑empty.
content.outputFormat: After each query, output the maximum element present in the array on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The sequence of operations follows the statement. After each operation the current maximum is printed, yielding the shown output.
All numbers are negative. The heap always keeps the largest (least negative) value at the top.
Mixed insertions and deletions demonstrate that after each deletion the next maximum is correctly reported.

solution.approach: Maintain a max‑heap (priority queue with reverse order). For a "+ x" query push x into the heap. For a "-" query pop the top element (the current maximum). After each operation the top of the heap is the required answer, which can be obtained in O(1). All operations are O(log size) and the total complexity per test case is O(N log N).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case (each insertion or deletion costs logarithmic time).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) – the heap stores at most N elements.
